Log \#3: 

Prompts: Review this slot machine app and improve its software engineering quality. Keep working features intact unless necessary. Refactor for meaningful names, smaller functions, less duplicate code, and clearer modular structure. Add JSDoc comments with type annotations for the main JavaScript functions. Help handle simple edge cases and errors. Add or suggest basic unit tests for the core game logic and token system. Also help prepare the code so it can pass linting and validation checks for HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

![:brain:][image1] Game Logic Changes (JS)

• **Logic extracted into separate module (game-logic.js)**

Core functions moved out of UI layer (spin, payout, cashout, mood, etc.)

• **Config-driven architecture introduced**

DEFAULT\_GAME\_CONFIG centralizes all tunable values (costs, payouts, symbols)

• **Pure functions for game flow**

startSpin, finishSpin, resolveCashOut, evaluateSpinResult

• **Stronger state validation**

Input sanitization (toWholeNumber, validation of reel symbols)

• **Explicit resolution objects**

Spin \+ cashout now return structured results (SpinResolution, CashOutResolution)

• **Error handling added**

Throws on invalid spins (e.g. not enough tokens)

UI catches failures and recovers gracefully

• **Bonus \+ pity logic moved into core engine**

No longer handled directly in UI

• **No gameplay changes**

Mechanics (payouts, bonus, pity) remain the same as iteration 2

![:test\_tube:][image2] Testing / Dev Infrastructure

• **Unit tests added (game-logic.test.mjs)**

Covers spin outcomes, bonus, pity refill, cashout

• **Package.json added**

Enables running tests via npm test

• **Code validation scripts**

check:js for syntax checking

![:video\_game:][image3] UX / Behavior Changes

• **Better error recovery**

Failed spins now handled without breaking UI

Shows fallback message (“machine jammed…”)

• **Feedback logic centralized**

UI derives feedback from spin resolution instead of inline logic

• **Cleaner effect handling**

Effects triggered via structured resolution (win/bonus/pity)

![:bricks:][image4] HTML Changes

• **Accessibility improvements**

Added aria-live, role="status" for dynamic updates

• **Added \<noscript\> banner**

Warns users if JS is disabled

• **Script now uses ES modules**

\<script type="module"\>

![:art:][image5] CSS Changes

• **Accessibility improvements**

Added prefers-reduced-motion support (disables animations)

• **Focus styles added**

Visible outlines for buttons (keyboard navigation)

• **Minor additions**

.noscript-banner styling

• **No major visual redesign**

Theme and layout largely unchanged from iteration 2

![:building\_construction:][image6] Architecture Shift (Biggest Change)

• From:

Single-file, UI-driven logic (iteration 2\)

• To:

**Modular, testable game engine \+ thin UI layer**

![:dart:][image7] Net Effect

Much more **maintainable and scalable**

Logic is now **testable and reusable**

UI is **decoupled from game rules**

Improved **robustness \+ accessibility**

* No major gameplay or visual changes — mostly **engineering quality upgrade**

**Validator output:**

**HTML**

1. **Error: The** `aria-label` **attribute must not be specified on any** `div` **element unless the element has a** `role` **value other than** `caption`**,** `code`**,** `deletion`**,** `emphasis`**,** `generic`**,** `insertion`**,** `paragraph`**,** `presentation`**,** `strong`**,** `subscript`**, or** `superscript`**.**  
   [From line 92, column 7; to line 92, column 60](https://validator.w3.org/nu/#l92c60)  
   `d">↩      <div class="scoreboard" aria-label="Token scoreboard">↩`    

**CSS**  
No errors

HTML Output: 

* In terms of output, the Token Furnace created from this run implemented a slot machine with specific functionalities. 

Needed Improvements: 

* Expanded theming and/or more options  
* Better indications of wins  
* Bonus meter should have a higher bar for more incentive
